volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Trinummus (Section 4.2.868b - Section 4.2.916e)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Trinummus: The Three Pieces of Money

sections.label: Section 4.2.868b
sections.original: Ad nostras aedis hic quidem habet rectam viam .
sections.translation: Surely this fellow is making in a straight line for my house ; i ’ faith , I think I shall have to keep watch this night of my arrival .

sections.label: Section 4.2.870
sections.original: Aperite hoc , aperite . heus , ecquis his fóribus tutelam gerit ?
sections.translation: Open this door !— open it ! Hallo , there ! who now has the care of this door [*] ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.871
sections.original: Quid , adulescens , quaeris ? quid vis ? quid istas pultas ?
sections.translation: Young man , what do you want ? hat is it you wish ? Why are you knocking at this door ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.871b
sections.translation: Eh ! old gentleman ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.872
sections.original: census cum sum , iuratori recte rationem dedi .
sections.translation: I gave the return correctly to the Censor [*] , when I was questioned by him —

sections.label: Section 4.2.873
sections.original: Lesbonicum hic adulescentem quaero in his regionibus
sections.translation: I am inquiring here for a young man named Lesbonicus , where in this quarter he lives — and likewise for another person , with such white hairs on his head as yours ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.875
sections.original: Calliclem aiebat vocari qui has dedit mi epistulas .
sections.translation: he that gave me these letters said his name was Callicles .

sections.label: Section 4.2.876
sections.original: Meum gnatum hic quidem Lesbonicum quaerit et amicum meum ,
sections.translation: In fact , this fellow is looking for my own son Lesbonicus and my friend Callicles , to whom I entrusted both my children and my property .

sections.label: Section 4.2.878
sections.original: Fac me , si scis , certiorem , hisce homines ubi habitent , pater .
sections.translation: Let me know , respected sir [*] , if you are acquainted with it , where these persons live .

sections.label: Section 4.2.879
sections.original: Quid eos quaeris ? aut quis es ? aut unde es ? aut unde advenis ?
sections.translation: Why are you inquiring for them ? Or who are you ?— Or whence are you ?— Or whence do you cone ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.880
sections.original: Multa simul rogitas , nescio quid expediam potissimum .
sections.translation: You ask a number of things in the same breath ; I know not which in especial to inform you upon . If you will ask each thing singly , and in a quiet manner , I ’ ll both let you know my name , and my business , and my travels .

sections.label: Section 4.2.883
sections.original: Faciam ita ut vis . agedum nomen tuom primum memora mihi .
sections.translation: I ’ ll do as you desire . Come then ; in the first place , tell me your name .

sections.label: Section 4.2.884
sections.translation: You begin by demanding an arduous task .

sections.label: Section 4.2.884b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.884c
sections.translation: Because , respected sir ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.885
sections.original: si ante lucem íre occipias a meo prímo nomine ,
sections.translation: if you were to begin before daylight , i ’ faith , to commence at the first part of my name [*] , ’ twould be the dead of the night before you could get to the end of it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.887
sections.original: Opus factost viatico ad tuom nomen , ut tu praedicas .
sections.translation: According to your story , a person should have a long journey ’ s provision crammed tightly in for your name .

sections.label: Section 4.2.888
sections.original: Est minusculum alterum , quasi vixillum vinarium .
sections.translation: I have another name somewhat less ,— about the size of a wine-casks [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.889
sections.translation: What is this name of yours , young man ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.889b
sections.translation: Hush , that ’ s my name [*] ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.890
sections.translation: that ’ s my every-day one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.890b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , ’ tis a scampish name ; just as though [*] you were to say , Hush , if I were confiding anything to you , and then it is at an end forthwith . ( Aside .) This fellow is evidently a sharper . What say you , young man —?

sections.label: Section 4.2.892b
sections.translation: What is it now ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.893
sections.original: Eloquere , isti tibi quid homines debent , quos tu quaeritas ?
sections.translation: Speak out ; what do these persons owe you whom you are seeking ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.894
sections.original: Pater istius adulescentis dedit has duas mi epistulas ,
sections.translation: The father of this young man , Lesbonicus , delivered to me these two letters ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.895
sections.translation: he is a friend of mine .

sections.label: Section 4.2.895b
sections.translation: I have now caught him in the fact ; he says that I gave him the letters . I will have some fine sport with the fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.2.897
sections.original: Ita ut occepi , si animum advortas , dicam .
sections.translation: As I have begun , if you will give attention , I will say on .

sections.label: Section 4.2.897b
sections.translation: I ’ ll give you my attention .

sections.label: Section 4.2.898
sections.original: Hanc me iussit Lesbonico suo gnato dare epistulam ,
sections.translation: He bade me give this letter to his son , Lesbonicus , and this other one , as well , he bade me give to his friend Callicles .

sections.label: Section 4.2.900
sections.original: Mihi quoque edepol , quom hic nugatur , contra nugari lubet .
sections.translation: Troth , but since he is acting the impostor , I , on the other hand , have an inclination to act the cheat as well . Where was he himself ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.901b
sections.translation: He was carrying on his business prosperously .

sections.label: Section 4.2.901c
sections.translation: But where ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.901d
sections.translation: At Seleucia .

sections.label: Section 4.2.902
sections.translation: ⋯ And did you receive these from himself ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.902b
sections.original: E manibus dedit mi ipse in manus .
sections.translation: With his own hands he himself delivered them into my hands .

sections.label: Section 4.2.903
sections.translation: Of what appearance is this person ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.903b
sections.translation: He is a person somewhere about half a foot taller than you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.904
sections.original: Haeret haec res , siquidem ego apsens sum quam praesens longior .
sections.translation: This is an odd matter , if in fact I am taller when absent than when present .

sections.label: Section 4.2.905
sections.translation: Do you know this person ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.905b
sections.translation: You are asking me a ridiculous question ; together with him I was in the habit of taking my meals .

sections.label: Section 4.2.906b
sections.translation: What is his name ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.906c
sections.translation: One , i ’ faith , that belongs to an honorable man .

sections.label: Section 4.2.907
sections.translation: I would like to hear it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.907b
sections.original: Illi edepol — illi — ílli — vae misero mihi .
sections.translation: Troth , his name ( hesitating ) — his — his — ( Aside .) Woe to unfortunate me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.908
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.908b
sections.translation: Unguardedly , I this moment swallowed the name .

sections.label: Section 4.2.909
sections.original: Non placet qui amicos intra dentes conclusos habet .
sections.translation: I like not the man that has his friends shut up within his teeth .

sections.label: Section 4.2.910
sections.original: Atque etiam modo vorsabatur mi in labris primoribus .
sections.translation: And yet this moment ’ twas dwelling on the very edge of my lips .

sections.label: Section 4.2.911
sections.translation: I ’ ve come to-day in good time before this fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.2.911b
sections.translation: To my sorrow I ’ m caught in the fact .

sections.label: Section 4.2.912
sections.translation: Have you now recollected the name ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.912b
sections.original: Deum hercle me atque hominum pudet .
sections.translation: ’ Fore Gods and men , i ’ faith , I ’ m ashamed of myself

sections.label: Section 4.2.913
sections.translation: See , now , how well you know this man .

sections.label: Section 4.2.913b
sections.original: Tam quam me . fieri istuc solet ,
sections.translation: As well as my own self . This is in the habit of happening : the thing you are holding in your hand , and seeing with your eyes , that same you are looking for as lost . I ’ ll recollect it letter by letter . C is the beginning of the name .

sections.label: Section 4.2.916
sections.translation: Is it Callias ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.916b
sections.translation: No : it isn ’ t that .

sections.label: Section 4.2.916c
sections.translation: Callippus ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.916d
sections.translation: It isn ’ t that .

sections.label: Section 4.2.916e
sections.translation: Callidemides ?
